# PZŁucz Rules — Research Document

> **Source:** REGULAMIN ŁUCZNICTWA Polskiego Związku Łuczniczego
> **Scope:** Sections §1–§4 (target archery, indoor, field, 3D, para-archery, appendices)
> **Purpose:** Comprehensive reference for the Advisor agent — maps every PZŁucz rule to ianseo concepts

---

## 1. Competition Formats

### 1.1 Disciplines (§1.1)

| Code | Polish Name                        | English        |
| ---- | ---------------------------------- | -------------- |
| TAR  | Łucznictwo tarczowe                | Target archery |
| HAL  | Łucznictwo halowe                  | Indoor archery |
| TER  | Łucznictwo terenowe                | Field archery  |
| 3D   | Łucznictwo 3D                      | 3D archery     |
| PARA | Łucznictwo dla osób z niepełnospr. | Para-archery   |
| RUN  | Łucznictwo biegowe                 | Run archery    |

### 1.2 Outdoor Target (§2.3.1)

**Qualification rounds** (§2.3.1.10):

| Round Name       | Arrows | Distances                      | Target Face | Categories                                        |
| ---------------- | ------ | ------------------------------ | ----------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| 70m Round        | 72     | 70m                            | 122cm       | R: M, W, U24M/W, U21M/W                           |
| 60m Round        | 72     | 60m                            | 122cm       | R: U18M/W, 50M/W                                  |
| 50m Round (C)    | 72     | 50m                            | 80cm        | C: all categories                                 |
| 50m Round (B)    | 72     | 50m                            | 122cm       | B: all categories                                 |
| 40m/20m Round    | 36+36  | 40m + 20m                      | see below   | U15M/W (all divisions)                            |
| 1440 Round       | 4×36   | 4 decreasing distances         | 122cm+80cm  | All (distances vary by category, see §2.3.1.10.6) |
| Double Round     | 2× any | Double of any above            | same        | Per organizer                                     |
| Half Round       | ½ any  | Half of rounds 2.3.1.10.1–10.5 | same        | Per organizer                                     |
| Children's Round | 4×18   | 4 distances (U12)              | 122cm+80cm  | U12                                               |

**40m/20m Round target details by division (§2.3.1.10.5):**

- Recurve: 122cm at 40m, 80cm at 20m
- Compound: 80cm at 40m, 60cm at 20m

**1440 Round distances by category (§2.3.1.10.6):**

- M, U24M, U21M: 90m, 70m, 50m, 30m
- W, U24W, U21W, U18M: 70m, 60m, 50m, 30m
- U18W: 60m, 50m, 40m, 30m
- Long distances → 122cm target; short distances → 80cm (6-ring) target

**End structure (§2.4.1.1):**

- Long distances & 50m/60m/70m round: 6-arrow ends
- Short distances: 3 or 6 arrows (6 mandatory at Polish Championships except U15/U12)
- U15 and U12: 3-arrow ends
- Elimination matches: 3-arrow ends

### 1.3 Indoor (§2.3.2)

**Qualification rounds (§2.3.2.7):**

| Round Name | Arrows | Distance | Target Face            | Categories        |
| ---------- | ------ | -------- | ---------------------- | ----------------- |
| 18m Round  | 60     | 18m      | Triple 40cm (vertical) | R, C: M/W/U24/U21 |
| 18m Round  | 60     | 18m      | Full 40cm              | R, C: U18; B: all |
| 18m Round  | 60     | 18m      | 60cm                   | U15               |
| 15m Round  | 60     | 15m      | 80cm                   | U12               |

**End structure:** 20 ends × 3 arrows (all categories).

### 1.4 Field Archery (§3.1, §3.4)

- 24 targets per round, 3 arrows per target = 72 arrows
- Target faces: field faces in 20cm, 40cm, 60cm, 80cm (scoring 1–6)
- Marked distances: specified per target size
- Unmarked distances: ranges differ per peg color:
  - **Red pegs** (R, C): longest ranges
  - **Blue pegs** (B, U18 R/C): medium ranges
  - **Yellow pegs** (U15 B): shortest ranges
- Championship round: 2 × 24 targets qualification (1 unmarked + 1 marked), top 16 elimination

**Field elimination (§3.4):**

- 1/8 and 1/4 finals: 6 marked targets
- 1/2 and finals: 4 marked targets
- Set system (R/B): 3 arrows per set, first to 6 set points
- Cumulative (C): 3 ends × 3 arrows, highest total wins

### 1.5 3D Archery (§3.2, §3.4)

- 24 targets (3D animal figures), 2 arrows per target in qualification = 48 arrows
- 1 arrow per target in elimination
- Unmarked distances only:
  - Red peg (R, C): max 45m, min 5m
  - Blue peg (B, T, L): max 30m, min 5m
- Championship round: 2 × 24 targets qualification, top 16 elimination (bracket §4.2.5)

**3D scoring zones:**

- 11 = small center
- 10 = large center
- 8 = vital zone
- 5 = body hit
- M = miss (also hooves, antlers)

### 1.6 Para-Archery (§2.10)

- Categories: R OPEN (M/W), C OPEN (M/W), W1 (M/W), VI1, VI2/3
- Same rounds as able-bodied except:
  - W1: 50m Round with full 80cm target; max draw weight 45 lbs; no peep sight, no magnifying lens, no level
  - VI: Special rounds at 30m (outdoor) and 18m (indoor) with 60/80/122cm targets; tactile sights only
- Teams: "Doubles" (2 athletes) instead of traditional 3-member teams
- Mixed teams: same structure as able-bodied
- Time: +10 seconds per arrow for all para categories

---

## 2. Divisions

### 2.1 Division Codes and Applicability (§1.3, §3.2)

| Code | Polish Name          | English     | Target | Indoor | Field | 3D  |
| ---- | -------------------- | ----------- | ------ | ------ | ----- | --- |
| R    | Łuk klasyczny        | Recurve     | ✓      | ✓      | ✓     | ✓   |
| C    | Łuk bloczkowy        | Compound    | ✓      | ✓      | ✓     | ✓   |
| B    | Łuk goły (Barebow)   | Barebow     | ✓      | ✓      | ✓     | ✓   |
| T    | Łuk tradycyjny       | Traditional | ✗      | ✗      | ✗     | ✓   |
| L    | Łuk prosty (Longbow) | Longbow     | ✗      | ✗      | ✗     | ✓   |

**Key equipment distinctions:**

- **Recurve (§2.2.1):** Risers + limbs, sights allowed, stabilizers allowed, clicker allowed, no magnifying lens
- **Compound (§2.2.2):** Compound bow, release aid mandatory, peep sight and scope with magnifying lens allowed, max 60 lbs
- **Barebow (§2.2.3):** No sight, no stabilizers, no clicker; face-walking and string-walking allowed; combined weight/dampener must pass 12.2cm ring test

### 2.2 Para-Archery Divisions

| Code | Name               | Notes                                            |
| ---- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------ |
| RMO  | R OPEN Men         | Same equipment as standard R                     |
| RWO  | R OPEN Women       | Same equipment as standard R                     |
| CMO  | C OPEN Men         | Same equipment as standard C                     |
| CWO  | C OPEN Women       | Same equipment as standard C                     |
| MW1  | W1 Men             | R or C bows; max 45 lbs; no peep/magnifier/level |
| WW1  | W1 Women           | Same as MW1                                      |
| VI1  | VI1 (Blind)        | Tactile sight only; must wear blindfold          |
| VI23 | VI2/3 (Low vision) | Tactile sight only; blindfold if merged with VI1 |

---

## 3. Age Classes

### 3.1 Age Categories (§1.2)

Age is determined by year of birth relative to competition year (not exact birthday).

| Code   | Polish Name     | English        | Age Rule                | Gender |
| ------ | --------------- | -------------- | ----------------------- | ------ |
| U12    | Dzieci          | Children       | ≤11 in competition year | M, W   |
| U15    | Młodzicy        | Cadets         | ≤14 in competition year | M, W   |
| U18    | Juniorzy młodsi | Cadets/U18     | ≤17 in competition year | M, W   |
| U21    | Juniorzy        | Juniors        | ≤20 in competition year | M, W   |
| U24    | Młodzieżowcy    | U24            | ≤23 in competition year | M, W   |
| Senior | Seniorzy        | Seniors (Open) | No age restriction      | M, W   |
| 50+    | Master          | Master         | ≥50 in competition year | M, W   |

### 3.2 Division × Age Availability

**Target & Indoor:**

- R: all age classes (U12 through 50+)
- C: U15, U18, U21, M/W, 50+ (no U12, no U24)
- B: U18, U21, M/W (no U12, U15, U24, 50+)

**Field:**

- R, C, B: standard age classes apply

**3D:**

- R, C, B, T, L: standard age classes; T and L only in 3D

**Note:** U24 has R only for target/indoor. No C or B for U24.

### 3.3 Full Categories Table (§1.4)

Complete category codes (Division + Age + Gender):

**Recurve:** RM, RW, RU24M, RU24W, RU21M, RU21W, RU18M, RU18W, RU15M, RU15W, RU12M, RU12W, R50M, R50W

**Compound:** CM, CW, CU21M, CU21W, CU18M, CU18W, CU15M, CU15W, C50M, C50W

**Barebow:** BM, BW, BU21M, BU21W, BU18M, BU18W

**3D only:** TM, TW, LM, LW

**Para:** RMO, RWO, CMO, CWO, MW1, WW1, VI1, VI2/3

---

## 4. Scoring Rules

### 4.1 Target Faces and Scoring Zones (§2.1.2)

**Outdoor target faces:**

| Face   | Diameter | Rings    | Used at                                        |
| ------ | -------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| 122cm  | 122cm    | 1–10 + X | Long distances (60–90m) for R; 50m for B       |
| 80cm   | 80cm     | 1–10 + X | Short distances; 50m for C (6-ring variant)    |
| 80cm/6 | 80cm     | 5–10 + X | Compound matchplay and short-distance compound |
| 60cm   | 60cm     | 1–10 + X | U15 compound at 20m                            |

**Indoor target faces:**

| Face        | Diameter | Rings    | Used at                                        |
| ----------- | -------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| Triple 40cm | 3×40cm   | 6–10 + X | Senior/U21/U24 R and C (vertical layout)       |
| Full 40cm   | 40cm     | 1–10 + X | U18 R/C; all B categories; U18 R/C elimination |
| 60cm        | 60cm     | 1–10 + X | U15 all divisions                              |
| 80cm        | 80cm     | 1–10 + X | U12 at 15m                                     |

**Compound-specific inner 10:**

- Outdoor: X-ring (inner 10) for tiebreaking
- Indoor: Inner 10 diameter = 2cm (compound); standard 10 = 4cm diameter (recurve)

**Field faces (§3.1):** Scoring 1–6 (6 = center bull). Sizes: 20cm, 40cm, 60cm, 80cm.

**3D figures (§3.2):** Scoring 11 (small center), 10 (large center), 8 (vital zone), 5 (body), M (miss/antlers/hooves).

### 4.2 Tiebreaking (§2.6.4)

**Outdoor target qualification tiebreaking (§2.6.4.1.1):**

1. Greater number of 10s (including X)
2. Greater number of Xs
3. If still tied → same rank position; coin toss for bracket seeding

**Indoor qualification tiebreaking (§2.6.4.1.2):**

1. Greater number of 10s
2. Greater number of 9s
3. If still tied → same rank position; coin toss for bracket seeding

**Elimination cut-in tiebreaker / critical positions (§2.6.4.2):**

- Shoot-off with 1 arrow at last shot distance
- Decided by value; if equal → closest to center; if equal → repeat

**Team shoot-off (§2.6.4.2.3):**

- 3 arrows (1 per member), or 2 arrows for mixed team (1 per member)
- Decided by sum; if equal → closest single arrow to center; then 2nd, 3rd closest

**Field tiebreaking:** Most 6s, then most 5s, then most 4s…

**3D tiebreaking:** Most 11s, then most 10s, then most 8s…

---

## 5. Qualification Formats

### 5.1 Outdoor Qualification Detail

All outdoor qualification rounds are shot in ends of 6 arrows (except U15/U12 who shoot 3-arrow ends).

| Categories           | Div | Distance | Arrows | Target   | Ends    |
| -------------------- | --- | -------- | ------ | -------- | ------- |
| M, W, U24M/W, U21M/W | R   | 70m      | 72     | 122cm    | 12 × 6  |
| U18M/W, 50M/W        | R   | 60m      | 72     | 122cm    | 12 × 6  |
| All C categories     | C   | 50m      | 72     | 80cm     | 12 × 6  |
| All B categories     | B   | 50m      | 72     | 122cm    | 12 × 6  |
| U15M/W               | R   | 40m+20m  | 36+36  | 122+80cm | 12+12×3 |
| U15M/W               | C   | 40m+20m  | 36+36  | 80+60cm  | 12+12×3 |

### 5.2 Indoor Qualification Detail

All indoor rounds: 20 ends × 3 arrows = 60 arrows.

| Categories           | Div  | Distance | Target      |
| -------------------- | ---- | -------- | ----------- |
| M, W, U24M/W, U21M/W | R, C | 18m      | Triple 40cm |
| U18M/W               | R, C | 18m      | Full 40cm   |
| All B categories     | B    | 18m      | Full 40cm   |
| U15M/W               | All  | 18m      | 60cm        |
| U12M/W               | R    | 15m      | 80cm        |

### 5.3 Field Qualification

- Championship: 2 × 24 targets (1 unmarked + 1 marked round)
- Each target: 3 arrows
- Total: 144 arrows

### 5.4 3D Qualification

- Championship: 2 × 24 targets
- Each target: 2 arrows (qualification)
- Total: 96 arrows

---

## 6. Elimination & Finals

### 6.1 Outdoor Individual Elimination (§2.3.1.1)

**Bracket:** Top 104 archers qualify → seeded per bracket diagram §4.2.1

**Match format by division:**

| Division  | System     | Ends | Arrows/end | Win condition         |
| --------- | ---------- | ---- | ---------- | --------------------- |
| Recurve   | Set system | ≤5   | 3          | First to 6 set points |
| Barebow   | Set system | ≤5   | 3          | First to 6 set points |
| Compound  | Cumulative | 5    | 3          | Highest total after 5 |
| W1 (para) | Cumulative | 5    | 3          | Highest total after 5 |

**Set system detail:** Win set → 2 set pts; Draw → 1 pt each; Lose → 0 pts. First to 6 pts wins.

**Shoot-off (tied after all ends):**

- 1 arrow each, highest value wins
- If same value → closest to center wins
- If equal distance → repeat

**Elimination distances (outdoor):**

| Categories           | Div | Distance | Target        |
| -------------------- | --- | -------- | ------------- |
| M, W, U24M/W, U21M/W | R   | 70m      | 122cm         |
| U18M/W               | R   | 60m      | 122cm         |
| U15M/W               | R   | 40m      | 122cm         |
| All                  | C   | 50m      | 80cm (6-ring) |
| All                  | B   | 50m      | 122cm         |

**Note (§2.3.1.8):** At Polish Championships, U15 has NO elimination round.

### 6.2 Outdoor Team Elimination (§2.3.1.2)

**Bracket:** Top 24 teams → seeded per bracket §4.2.2

**Team composition (§2.3.1.2.3):**

- 3 or 4 members registered before qualification starts
- 3 shoot per end; 4th member substitutes between ends
- Fixed roster for entire competition

**Match format:**

| Division | System     | Ends | Arrows/end       | Win condition         |
| -------- | ---------- | ---- | ---------------- | --------------------- |
| R, B     | Set system | ≤4   | 6 (2 per archer) | First to 5 set pts    |
| C, W1    | Cumulative | 4    | 6 (2 per archer) | Highest total after 4 |

### 6.3 Mixed Team Elimination

- Best-ranked man + best-ranked woman from same club
- Max 3 mixed teams per club (Polish Championships only; no limit in U15)
- Bracket: top 16 mixed teams
- Match: Set system (R/B) with 4 arrows/end (2 per archer), best of 4 sets, first to 5; Cumulative (C/W1) with 4 ends × 4 arrows

### 6.4 Indoor Individual Elimination (§2.3.2.1)

**Bracket:** Top 32 archers → seeded per bracket §4.2.4

Same set/cumulative system as outdoor.

**Elimination target faces (indoor, §2.3.2.3–2.3.2.5):**

| Categories           | Div  | Distance | Target               |
| -------------------- | ---- | -------- | -------------------- |
| M, W, U24M/W, U21M/W | R, C | 18m      | Triple 40cm (linear) |
| U18M/W               | R, C | 18m      | Full 40cm            |
| All                  | B    | 18m      | Full 40cm            |
| U15M/W               | All  | 18m      | 60cm                 |

### 6.5 Indoor Team Elimination (§2.3.2.2)

**Bracket:** Top 16 teams → seeded per bracket §4.2.5

Same structure as outdoor teams (3 or 4 members, set/cumulative system).

### 6.6 Field/3D Elimination (§3.4)

**Bracket:** Top 16 individuals → seeded bracket

**Field elimination match:**

- 1/8 and 1/4: 6 marked targets, 3 arrows each
- 1/2 and Finals: 4 marked targets, 3 arrows each
- R/B: Set system (first to 6 set pts, 3 arrows per set)
- C: Cumulative (3 ends × 3 arrows)

**3D elimination match:**

- 1/8 and 1/4: 6 targets, 1 arrow each
- 1/2 and Finals: 4 targets, 1 arrow each
- Same set/cumulative rules

### 6.7 Finals Format (§2.5.3)

**Semi-finals and medal matches** are shot on a separate finals field with:

- Alternate shooting (one archer at a time)
- 20 seconds per arrow individual; 2 minutes for 6 team arrows
- Individual athletes have separate targets (no sharing)
- Agents (not athletes) retrieve arrows and verify scoring

### 6.8 Post-Elimination Classification (§2.6.5)

Losers within same round classified by:

1. Set points (for set system) or total points (for cumulative)
2. If tie → qualification ranking
3. If still tie → same rank position

---

## 7. Team Composition

### 7.1 Standard Teams (§2.3.1.2.3)

- **Size:** 3 members or 3+1 substitute
- **Requirement:** All from same club, same division, same age category
- **Roster lock:** Registered before qualification starts; fixed for entire competition
- **Substitution:** In 4-member team, can swap between ends (not within an end)
- **Scoring rule:** Best 3 of 4 qualification scores determine team ranking

### 7.2 Mixed Teams (§2.3.1.2.4)

- **Size:** 1 man + 1 woman from same club
- **Selection:** Best-ranked man + best-ranked woman form Team 1; 2nd best + 2nd best form Team 2; etc.
- **Limit:** Max 3 mixed teams per club at Polish Championships (no limit for U15)
- **Roster change:** Manager can change composition up to 30 minutes before elimination starts
- **Medals:** Awarded to the pair that actually competes in elimination

### 7.3 Field/3D Teams (§3.4)

**Field team ranking:** Best R + Best C + Best B from same club (sum of qualification scores)

**3D team ranking:** Best C + Best B + Best T + Best L from same club

---

## 8. Rankings & Classifications

### 8.1 Qualification Ranking

- Ranked by total score
- Tiebreaking per §4.2 (see Section 4.2 above)
- Teams ranked by sum of 3 best individual scores (or 2 for mixed teams)

### 8.2 Records (§2.11)

Records are tracked per category for many round types. Key points:

- Minimum 3 judges required (at least 1 Class-I or National)
- Must be at WA-ranked competition
- Application within 10 days with scorecards
- Field/3D have NO records (§3.14)

**Record categories include:**

- Individual rounds: 70m, 60m, 50m, 1440, double rounds, half rounds, 18m, 40m/20m
- Team rounds: same round types + team/mixed team variants
- Special shootings: rooster, sunflower, clover, poppy (R only, at Polish Championships)

### 8.3 Special Shootings (§2.12)

Unique PZŁucz tradition at Polish Championships for Recurve individual medalists:

- Distance: 15m
- 5 ends × 3 arrows
- Special 32cm-diameter target (placed on 80cm face, fields 7–10), with 2cm white center + 1mm cross
- Scoring: count hits in 32cm zone; tiebreak by sum of distances from center
- Themed targets by age:
  - Senior → Rooster (kur)
  - Junior (U21) → Sunflower (słonecznik)
  - Junior younger (U18) → Clover (koniczyna)
  - Cadet (U15) → Poppy (mak)

---

## 9. Time Controls

### 9.1 Qualification Time Limits (§2.5.5)

| Shooting mode                  | Time per arrow |
| ------------------------------ | -------------- |
| Individual simultaneous        | 30 seconds     |
| Individual alternating (elim.) | 20 seconds     |
| Team alternating               | 20 seconds     |
| Para: all above                | +10 seconds    |

Calculated totals: e.g., 6-arrow end simultaneous = 180s; 3-arrow end simultaneous = 90s.

### 9.2 Field/3D Time Limits (§3.6.8)

| Event            | Time  |
| ---------------- | ----- |
| Field qual.      | 3 min |
| Field elim.      | 2 min |
| Field mixed team | 160s  |
| 3D qual.         | 2 min |
| 3D elim.         | 1 min |
| Team field/3D    | 2 min |
| 3D mixed team    | 90s   |

---

## 10. ianseo Mapping (Cross-Reviewed)

> **Cross-review note:** This section was refined after analysis of the actual
> ianseo source code (`FITA/lib.php`, `Modules/Sets/lib.php`, `FITA/Setup_Target.php`).
> All division codes, class codes, bracket phases, and match mode values have
> been verified against the codebase.

### 10.1 Tournament Types → ianseo Type IDs

| PZŁucz Round           | ianseo Type ID         | Setup Script             | Notes                                                                                       |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------- | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 70m Round (R)          | **3** (70m Round)      | `Setup_3_PL.php`         | Direct match. FITA's `Setup_3.php` is the reference. PL needs custom classes/divisions.     |
| 60m Round (R U18/50+)  | **10** (720 Round 60m) | `Setup_10_PL.php`        | Type 10 = 72 arrows at 60m. Same arrow count/structure; PL adds U18/50+ specific config.    |
| 50m Round (C)          | **9** (720 Round 50m)  | `Setup_9_PL.php`         | Type 9 = 72 arrows at 50m. PL needs 80cm target (6-ring for compound matchplay).            |
| 50m Round (B)          | **9** (720 Round 50m)  | `Setup_9_PL.php`         | Same type as compound but with 122cm full face. Handled via sub-rule or target face config. |
| 40m/20m Round (U15)    | **3** (via sub-rule)   | `Setup_3_PL.php`         | Split-distance round. Use `CreateDistanceNew()` with 2 distances + different target faces.  |
| 1440 Round             | **1** (FITA/1440)      | `Setup_1_PL.php`         | Direct match. Distance table varies by PZŁucz category — needs PL-specific distances.       |
| Indoor 18m             | **6** (Indoor 18)      | `Setup_6_PL.php`         | Direct match. PL needs different target faces per category (triple 40/full 40/60/80cm).     |
| Indoor 15m (U12)       | **6** (via sub-rule)   | `Setup_6_PL.php`         | Use type 6 with distance override to 15m for U12 class. Custom distance info entry.         |
| Indoor 25m             | **7** (Indoor 25)      | `Setup_7_PL.php` or FITA | Direct match. Low priority — consider using FITA default.                                   |
| Field Unmarked         | **11**                 | `Setup_11_PL.php`        | ianseo has `TGT_FIELD` target type and field tournament types 11/12/13. Needs PL classes.   |
| Field Marked           | **12**                 | `Setup_12_PL.php`        | Same as above for marked distances.                                                         |
| Field Mixed            | **13**                 | `Setup_13_PL.php`        | Combined unmarked + marked round.                                                           |
| 3D Round               | CUSTOM (new type?)     | `Setup_XX_PL.php`        | 3D uses `TGT_3D`. May need a new type or reuse existing if available.                       |
| Children's Round (U12) | **1** (via sub-rule)   | `Setup_1_PL.php`         | 4×18 arrows. Handle as a sub-rule of 1440 Round with reduced distances.                     |
| Special Shootings      | CUSTOM                 | Separate module          | Unique to PZŁucz. Requires custom scoring module (not a standard tournament type).          |

**Key implementation insight:** Most PZŁucz rounds map to existing ianseo type IDs.
The Setup script `Setup_{type}_PL.php` customizes divisions, classes, distances,
and target faces within the existing type framework. The rare truly custom types
are 3D (if no existing type ID covers it) and Special Shootings.

### 10.2 Divisions → `CreateDivision()` Parameters

FITA uses exactly these division codes (verified in `FITA/lib.php`):

| PZŁucz Div   | ianseo Code | `CreateDivision()` Call                                           | Status   |
| ------------ | ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- |
| R (Recurve)  | `'R'`       | `CreateDivision($TourId, 1, 'R', 'Recurve')`                      | Standard |
| C (Compound) | `'C'`       | `CreateDivision($TourId, 2, 'C', 'Compound')`                     | Standard |
| B (Barebow)  | `'B'`       | `CreateDivision($TourId, 3, 'B', 'Barebow')`                      | Standard |
| T (Trad.)    | `'T'`       | `CreateDivision($TourId, 4, 'T', 'Traditional')`                  | FITA 3D  |
| L (Longbow)  | `'L'`       | `CreateDivision($TourId, 5, 'L', 'Longbow')`                      | FITA 3D  |
| Para R OPEN  | `'RO'`      | `CreateDivision($TourId, n, 'RO', 'Recurve OPEN', 1, '', '', 1)`  | Custom   |
| Para C OPEN  | `'CO'`      | `CreateDivision($TourId, n, 'CO', 'Compound OPEN', 1, '', '', 1)` | Custom   |
| Para W1      | `'W1'`      | `CreateDivision($TourId, n, 'W1', 'W1', 1, '', '', 1)`            | Custom   |
| Para VI      | `'VI'`      | `CreateDivision($TourId, n, 'VI', 'VI', 1, '', '', 1)`            | Custom   |

**Note:** R, C, B, T, L are all FITA-standard codes — the pzlucz-rules.md previously
listed 'OL', 'CO', 'BB' which were **incorrect**. Para divisions need `$IsPara=1`.

### 10.3 Age Classes → `CreateClass()` Parameters

FITA's `CreateStandardClasses()` already creates many of these (verified in `FITA/lib.php`):

| PZŁucz Class | ianseo Code | `CreateClass()` Key Params                                                    | Status              |
| ------------ | ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------- |
| Senior M     | `'M'`       | `CreateClass($TourId, 1, 21, 49, 0, 'M', 'M', 'Men')`                         | FITA standard       |
| Senior W     | `'W'`       | `CreateClass($TourId, 2, 21, 49, 1, 'W', 'W', 'Women')`                       | FITA standard       |
| U24 M        | `'U24M'`    | `CreateClass($TourId, n, 21, 23, 0, 'U24M', 'U24M,M', 'Under 24 Men')`        | **PL custom** (new) |
| U24 W        | `'U24W'`    | `CreateClass($TourId, n, 21, 23, 1, 'U24W', 'U24W,W', 'Under 24 Women')`      | **PL custom** (new) |
| U21 M        | `'U21M'`    | `CreateClass($TourId, 3, 18, 20, 0, 'U21M', 'U21M,M', 'Under 21 Men')`        | FITA standard       |
| U21 W        | `'U21W'`    | `CreateClass($TourId, 4, 18, 20, 1, 'U21W', 'U21W,W', 'Under 21 Women')`      | FITA standard       |
| U18 M        | `'U18M'`    | `CreateClass($TourId, 5, 15, 17, 0, 'U18M', 'U18M,U21M,M', 'Under 18 Men')`   | FITA standard       |
| U18 W        | `'U18W'`    | `CreateClass($TourId, 6, 15, 17, 1, 'U18W', 'U18W,U21W,W', 'Under 18 Women')` | FITA standard       |
| U15 M        | `'U15M'`    | `CreateClass($TourId, 7, 1, 14, 0, 'U15M', 'U15M,U18M,U21M,M', ...)`          | FITA (types 3,37)   |
| U15 W        | `'U15W'`    | `CreateClass($TourId, 8, 1, 14, 1, 'U15W', 'U15W,U18W,U21W,W', ...)`          | FITA (types 3,37)   |
| U12 M        | `'U12M'`    | `CreateClass($TourId, n, 1, 11, 0, 'U12M', 'U12M,U15M,...', ...)`             | **PL custom** (new) |
| U12 W        | `'U12W'`    | `CreateClass($TourId, n, 1, 11, 1, 'U12W', 'U12W,U15W,...', ...)`             | **PL custom** (new) |
| Master M     | `'50M'`     | `CreateClass($TourId, 9, 50, 100, 0, '50M', '50M,M', '50+ Men')`              | FITA standard       |
| Master W     | `'50W'`     | `CreateClass($TourId, 10, 50, 100, 1, '50W', '50W,W', '50+ Women')`           | FITA standard       |

**Key insight:** The `$ValidClass` parameter (5th) is a comma-separated list of class
codes the archer "belongs to" for event eligibility. E.g., a U18 archer has
`'U18M,U21M,M'` meaning they can compete at the U18, U21, or Open level. PL must
set these chains correctly to enable upward eligibility.

**Age ranges** use "years from now" calculation: `$From`/`$To` are relative age
boundaries, NOT birth years. E.g., `15, 17` means ages 15–17.

**Truly custom classes:** Only **U24** (no WA equivalent) and **U12** (below FITA's
minimum). U15 and 50+ are already in FITA for certain tournament types.

### 10.4 Elimination Brackets → `EvFinalFirstPhase`

The bracket size is controlled by `EvFinalFirstPhase` in `CreateEventNew()`.
This maps to `PhId` values in the `Phases` table. Verified actual values from
`FITA/lib.php`:

| PZŁucz Bracket        | `EvFinalFirstPhase` | Archers in bracket | Status         |
| --------------------- | ------------------- | ------------------ | -------------- |
| 104 outdoor indiv.    | **48**              | 104 (48 matches)   | **EXISTING** ✓ |
| 32 indoor indiv.      | **16**              | 32                 | **EXISTING** ✓ |
| 24 outdoor team       | **12**              | 24 (12 matches)    | **EXISTING** ✓ |
| 16 indoor team        | **8**               | 16                 | **EXISTING** ✓ |
| 16 mixed/field indiv. | **8**               | 16                 | **EXISTING** ✓ |

**Critical correction:** The previous version labeled 104-draw and 24-draw brackets
as "CUSTOM". In fact, FITA's `lib.php` already uses `$FirstPhase = 48` for outdoor
individual and `$TeamFirstPhase = 12` for outdoor teams. ianseo's `Grids` and
`Phases` tables handle these bracket sizes natively. **No custom bracket code needed.**

### 10.5 Match Scoring Mode → `EvMatchMode`

Match scoring is controlled by `EvMatchMode` in `CreateEventNew()`. There are
**no** `MATCH_SET` or `MATCH_CUMULATIVE` constants — the previous version was
incorrect.

| PZŁucz Rule                 | `EvMatchMode` | Additional Options                                                           |
| --------------------------- | ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Set system (R/B individual) | **1**         | `EvElimEnds=5, EvElimArrows=3, EvElimSO=1`                                   |
| Cumulative (C individual)   | **0**         | `EvElimEnds=5, EvElimArrows=3, EvElimSO=1`                                   |
| Set system (R/B team)       | **1**         | `EvElimEnds=4, EvElimArrows=6, EvElimSO=3, EvMaxTeamPerson=3`                |
| Cumulative (C team)         | **0**         | `EvElimEnds=4, EvElimArrows=6, EvElimSO=3, EvMaxTeamPerson=3`                |
| Set system (R/B mixed team) | **1**         | `EvElimEnds=4, EvElimArrows=4, EvElimSO=2, EvMaxTeamPerson=2, EvMixedTeam=1` |
| Cumulative (C mixed team)   | **0**         | Same structure as above with `EvMatchMode=0`                                 |

**Note:** `MATCH_*` constants (`MATCH_ALL_SEP`, `MATCH_NO_SEP`, etc.) control
**match separation** (whether adjacent targets see each other's scores), not
the scoring system. These have values 0–255 as a bitmask.

### 10.6 Target Faces → `TGT_*` Constants & `CreateTargetFace()`

| PZŁucz Target                   | `TGT_*` Constant    | Val | `CreateTargetFace()` `$Classes`         | Notes                                                   |
| ------------------------------- | ------------------- | --- | --------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| 122cm full (outdoor R/B)        | `TGT_OUT_FULL`      | 5   | `'REG-R.','REG-B.'`                     | Standard outdoor face for recurve/barebow               |
| 80cm full (outdoor C qual)      | `TGT_OUT_FULL`      | 5   | `'REG-C.'`                              | Full 10-ring, 80cm diameter                             |
| 80cm 6-ring (outdoor C elim)    | `TGT_OUT_6_big10`   | 10  | `'REG-C.'` (events only)                | Compound matchplay face; size set via `EvTargetSize=80` |
| Triple 40cm (indoor R/C senior) | `TGT_IND_6_big10`   | 2   | `'RM,RW,RU24M,RU24W,RU21M,RU21W,CM,CW'` | Vertical triple; different inner-10 for C               |
| Triple 40cm small-10 (indoor C) | `TGT_IND_6_small10` | 4   | `'CM,CW,CU21M,CU21W'`                   | Compound inner-10 = 2cm (vs 4cm recurve)                |
| Full 40cm (indoor U18/B)        | `TGT_IND_1_big10`   | 1   | `'REG-[RCB]U18.','REG-B.'`              | Single 40cm face, standard 10-ring                      |
| 60cm (indoor U15)               | `TGT_OUT_FULL`      | 5   | `'REG-.U15.'`                           | 60cm diameter; may need size param                      |
| 80cm (indoor U12 at 15m)        | `TGT_OUT_FULL`      | 5   | `'REG-.U12.'`                           | 80cm full face at 15m                                   |
| Field faces (20/40/60/80cm)     | `TGT_FIELD`         | 6   | All field classes                       | Scoring zones 1–6                                       |
| 3D figures                      | `TGT_3D`            | 8   | All 3D classes                          | Scoring 11/10/8/5/M                                     |

**Note on `EvTargetSize`:** The physical target diameter is set separately via
`EvTargetSize` in `CreateEventNew()`. `TGT_*` constants define the scoring ring
layout, not the physical size. E.g., both 122cm and 80cm outdoor faces use
`TGT_OUT_FULL` but with different `EvTargetSize` values.

**Note on Golds/X counting:** Set via `EvGolds`, `EvXNine`, `EvGoldsChars`,
`EvXNineChars` in `CreateEventNew()`:

- Outdoor: `EvGolds='10+X', EvXNine='X', EvGoldsChars='KL', EvXNineChars='K'`
- Indoor: `EvGolds='10', EvXNine='9', EvGoldsChars='L', EvXNineChars='J'`

---

## 11. Gaps & Custom Needs (Cross-Reviewed)

> **Cross-review note:** Re-assessed after verifying the ianseo API. Several
> items previously marked as "CUSTOM" are actually supported natively.
> Each gap is now rated for implementation difficulty and priority.

### 11.1 U24 Age Class — **Priority: HIGH | Difficulty: LOW**

U24 (Młodzieżowcy, 21–23 years) has no WA equivalent. Must be created via
`CreateClass()` in every PL setup script. Simple addition — just define the
age range and valid-class chain (`'U24M,M'` / `'U24W,W'`). Only applies to
Recurve division per PZŁucz rules.

### 11.2 U12 Age Class & Children's Round — **Priority: MEDIUM | Difficulty: MEDIUM**

U12 (Dzieci, ≤11 years) is below FITA's minimum. Needs:

- Custom class definition with age range `(1, 11)` and valid chain `'U12M,U15M,...'`
- Children's Round: 4×18 arrows at 4 distances — handle as sub-rule of type 1 (1440)
  or as a distinct setup configuration within `Setup_1_PL.php`
- Indoor U12: 15m instead of 18m — custom distance info entry in `Setup_6_PL.php`

### 11.3 Split-Distance Rounds (40m/20m) — **Priority: HIGH | Difficulty: LOW**

`CreateDistanceNew()` already supports multiple distances per category via the
`$Distances` array parameter. The 40m/20m round for U15 is handled by creating
two distance entries with different target face configurations. Not a custom
feature — just a specific setup script configuration.

### 11.4 Special Shootings (§2.12) — **Priority: LOW | Difficulty: HIGH**

Completely unique to PZŁucz. Requires a custom module with:

- Custom target face (32cm on 80cm background, themed artwork)
- Custom scoring (count hits in zone + distance-from-center tiebreak)
- Integration with medal winners from main competition
- Separate page/menu entry under `PRNT` or custom category

This is a standalone feature module, not part of the standard setup flow.
Defer until all core tournament types are working.

### 11.5 Para-Archery Doubles — **Priority: LOW | Difficulty: MEDIUM**

PZŁucz uses 2-member "doubles" teams for para-archery instead of 3-member.
Implementation: use `EvMaxTeamPerson=2` (same as mixed teams) with
para-specific divisions. The `CreateEventNew()` API already supports arbitrary
team sizes via `EvMaxTeamPerson`.

### 11.6 Field/3D Course Management — **Priority: MEDIUM | Difficulty: MEDIUM**

ianseo already has tournament types 11/12/13 for field and `TGT_FIELD`/`TGT_3D`
target types. The PL module needs setup scripts that create PZŁucz-specific
classes and divisions for these types. The variable-distance course and peg-color
routing are managed operationally (not in ianseo software). The scoring system
is natively supported by `TGT_FIELD` (1–6 zones) and `TGT_3D` (11/10/8/5/M).

### 11.7 Indoor Compound Inner-10 — **Priority: HIGH | Difficulty: LOW**

Handled natively by ianseo's target face system. Use `TGT_IND_6_small10` (val 4)
for compound indoor triple face (2cm inner-10) vs. `TGT_IND_6_big10` (val 2) for
recurve indoor triple face (4cm inner-10). Already differentiated in FITA's setup
as `$TargetC` vs `$TargetR`. No custom code needed — just correct target face
assignment in `CreateTargetFace()`.

### 11.8 Best 3 of 4 Team Scoring — **Priority: HIGH | Difficulty: MEDIUM**

In polish rules, teams are defined before the start of the competition, and
can be composed from 3 or 4 athletes. However, their qualification result is
based on sum of only 3 best athletes from given team.

### 11.9 Mixed Team Formation Rules — **Priority: MEDIUM | Difficulty: MEDIUM**

Automatic matching (best M + best W → Team 1, etc.) and the 3-team-per-club
limit at Polish Championships require custom logic. The `InsertClassEvent()`
API supports mixed team events (`EvMixedTeam=1`), but the auto-formation and
club limit enforcement would need a custom rank override or event processing
script.

### 11.10 PZŁucz-Specific Tiebreaking — **Priority: HIGH | Difficulty: LOW**

Indoor tiebreaking differs from outdoor (10s then 9s, vs 10s then Xs). This is
controlled by `EvGoldsChars`/`EvXNineChars` in `CreateEventNew()` and the gold/X
counting per target face in `TargetFaceGoldsXnines()`. Correct configuration in
the setup script handles this — no custom ranking code needed for standard
tiebreaks.

### 11.11 Non-Standard Elimination Brackets — **REMOVED**

~~Previously listed as a gap.~~ Verified that ianseo natively supports all PZŁucz
bracket sizes: 104-draw (`EvFinalFirstPhase=48`), 24-draw team (`12`), 32-draw
(`16`), and 16-draw (`8`). No custom bracket code needed.

### 11.12 Traditional & Longbow Divisions — **REMOVED**

~~Previously listed as custom.~~ FITA's `CreateStandardDivisions('3D')` already
creates `'L'` (Longbow) and `'T'` (Traditional) divisions. PL 3D setup scripts
can call this directly.

---

## Appendix A — Bracket Reference

| Bracket  | Usage                             | PDF Section |
| -------- | --------------------------------- | ----------- |
| 104-draw | Outdoor individual                | §4.2.1      |
| 64-draw  | Alternative individual            | §4.2.1 alt  |
| 24-draw  | Outdoor team                      | §4.2.2      |
| 32-draw  | Indoor individual                 | §4.2.4      |
| 16-draw  | Indoor team, Mixed team, Field/3D | §4.2.5      |

## Appendix B — Dress Code Summary (§2.7, §3.13)

- Club shirts required at all national events
- Full matching team uniform for team matches and medal matches
- No jeans, no camouflage patterns, no oversized/baggy shorts
- Shorts not shorter than fingertip length
- Shirts must cover front, back, both shoulders, diaphragm at full draw
- Field/3D: no camo on any equipment or clothing
